review_title: Great benefits from Omega fish oil!
review_text: I once ordered the Now brand omega fish oil, and it was great because of the quantity it provides. However I would like to try this brand California gold nutrition on this kind of omega fish oil which is in TG ( Triglycerides)form and way more effective and bioavailable rather than EE ( Ethyl Esters) that are mostly found in cheaper omega fish oil brands! TG is the natural form found in fish, offering higher absorption and stability. EE is a processed, concentrated form used in supplements to boost EPA/DHA levels, often cheaper, but less bioavailable and more prone to oxidation. Key Differences: Structure: TG consists of three fatty acids attached to a glycerol backbone, whereas EE binds fatty acids to an ethanol molecule. Bioavailability: Studies suggest TG fish oils are better absorbed, with some evidence showing 50% higher plasma concentration of EPA and DHA compared to EE forms. Concentration & Processing: EE forms are created by distilling fish oil to remove impurities and increase omega-3 concentration, making them ideal for high-strength supplements. Stability: EE is less stable than natural TG and more prone to oxidation. Re-esterified TG (rTG): This is a high-end option where EE is converted back to a TG form, offering the concentration benefits of EE with the high bioavailability of natural TG. Which to Choose? While both provide benefits, TG (or rTG) is generally considered superior for absorption, but EE is often used in high-potency, affordable, or prescription-strength formulas.

An omega-3 supplement is a dietary product that provides omega-3 fatty acids, essential fats that the body needs for optimal health but cannot produce on its own. These supplements typically contain EPA (eicosapentaenoic acid) and DHA (docosahexaenoic acid), which are derived from fish oil, krill oil, or algae oil. Some supplements may also include ALA (alpha-linolenic acid) from plant sources. Omega-3 supplements are popular for supporting heart health, brain function, eye health, and reducing inflammation. They are often used by individuals who do not consume enough omega-3-rich foods like fatty fish, flaxseeds, or walnuts.

Here are some important aspects to consider when evaluating the quality and effectiveness of omega-3 fish oil: Quality: Look for a reputable brand that uses high-quality ingredients and follows strict quality control measures during production. The higher the concentration of these fatty acids, the more potent the supplement will be. The source of the fish oil is also important. It's best to choose a product made from small, cold-water fish that are low on the food chain, as they are less likely to accumulate toxins. Additionally, look for a supplement made from fresh fish oil, as the quality of the oil can deteriorate over time.

Basic Concept: Fish oil supplements are primarily valued for their high content of Omega-3 fatty acids, specifically Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) and Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA). These are essential polyunsaturated fats that the human body cannot efficiently produce on its own. Professional Definition & Why It Matters: Professionally, Omega-3s are known for their anti-inflammatory properties and their crucial roles in cardiovascular health (helping to maintain healthy triglyceride levels and blood pressure) and neurological function (DHA is a major structural component of the brain and retina). My Experience: Joint Comfort: I noticed a definite reduction in morning stiffness, which is what initially motivated me to try fish oil. My knees feel significantly more comfortable during daily walks. Cognitive Function: I feel a subtle but positive lift in my mental clarity and sustained focus throughout the afternoon. The brand seems to purify the oil very effectively. Conclusion: This product delivers on its promises regarding essential fatty acid intake. Dietary supplements can be a great source of nutrients. They can help improve your overall health and may reduce your risk of some health conditions. Sometimes, a healthcare professional might recommend that you take a dietary supplement. For instance, they might advise that you take an iron supplement if you have iron deficiency anemia or take calcium if you a have risk of osteoporosis. A healthcare professional might recommend a dietary supplement if you: have a high risk of a health condition that can be lowered with the addition of a nutrient need extra nutrients because you’re pregnant or nursing have a condition such as kidney failure that makes it difficult for your body to absorb nutrients have a specialized or restricted diet that lacks a specific nutrient experience malnutrition and don’t get enough nutrients from your diet receive lab results showing a deficiency of a specific nutrient are an older adult and have trouble absorbing nutrients from foods Dietary supplements can help you improve or maintain your overall health, and supplements can also help you meet your daily requirements of essential nutrients. For example, calcium and vitamin D can help build strong bones, and fiber can help to maintain bowel regularity.

The three main omega-3 fatty acids are alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA), and docosahexaenoic acid (DHA). ALA is found mainly in plant oils such as flaxseed, soybean, and canola oils. DHA and EPA are found in fish and other seafood. ALA is an essential fatty acid, meaning that your body can’t make it, so you must get it from the foods and beverages you consume. Your body can convert some ALA into EPA and then to DHA, but only in very small amounts. Therefore, getting EPA and DHA from foods (and dietary supplements if you take them) is the only practical way to increase levels of these omega-3 fatty acids in your body.

What's most impressive about these enteric coated soft gels is that you use less and save more.  Better than that, you get Twice the Potency compared to most other fish oil products out there on the market.  The only way to compare price is by comparing the serving size. Some fish oil products will offer 180 at the same price here but you have to take 3 or even 4 soft gels per dosage to get the same equivalent. Many of them also are not coated soft gels, which in this case it really does reduce or eliminate those annoying fish burps. Granted the pills are HUGE as compared to a standard vitamin D3 soft gel, which my doctor recommends you take in conjunction with fish oil for faster absorption into the bloodstream. (SEE PIC) The downside to this purchase like all other fish oil products is that although they're Made in the USA other ingredients are SOURCED from other places from around the world. My understanding is that Nature's Bounty fish oil, according to their website, comes from fisheries in Peru. I didn't know that Peru was specifically sought out by Nature's Bounty as their exclusive source for fish oil. The Marine Research Institute (IMARPE), a HIGHLY recognized world-class authority, works with the Peruvian government to manage the controls of fish stock along their waterways. Smaller boats are used exclusively in the fishery process which proves more effective and most importantly, no facility is allowed to receive fish from vessels without a valid fishery license. All catches in Peru are carefully reviewed to meet RIGOROUS QUALITY CHECKS. As much as I would prefer fish sourced from the US or Canada exclusively, I realize that isn't always going to be a realistic expectation. Peru appears to be at least for now, a more SAFER alternative. I also typically try to order my fish oil by mail during cooler months as I realize prolonged exposure to heat in transit can also degrade the potency of any fish oil product. However, this deal offered at Amazon was simply too good to pass up. The expiration date of my bottle shows 10/23. (SEE PIC) Not bad as I've ordered vitamins in the past and been very unhappy with much shorter expiration dates.. I read online here that some reviewers prefer pharmaceutical grade fish oil exclusively. However, that term is not an official FDA term nor an official medical term when applied to the sale of fish oil products in the US. However, the price on such fish oil is multiplied by 100 as compared to what I paid here today. Personally, I'd rather trust Nature's Bounty which I've been using for over 10 years to continue to get the job done. They haven't disappointed me yet. Trust me friends. I did the research. Honestly, you wont be disappointed.
